channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: St Kilda’s 2026 AFL season: performance analysis shows mixed results despite major recruiting drive
item.description: St Kilda’s 2026 AFL campaign is under scrutiny following an aggressive off-season recruiting spree aimed at pushing the club into finals contention. Despite high-profile acquisitions including Tom De ...

item.title: President Ruto, First Lady Rachel and Family Attend Historic Madaraka Day Celebrations in Wajir
item.description: President William Ruto, together with First Lady Rachel Ruto, attended the 63rd Madaraka Day celebrations held on Monday, 1 June 2026, at the newly upgraded 10,000-seater Wajir Stadium in Wajir County...

item.title: Angeles City building collapse: 12th fatality confirmed as rescue operations continue
item.description: Rescue operations in Angeles City, Pampanga continued after the collapse of a nine-storey building under construction, with authorities confirming that the death toll has risen to 12 following the rec...

item.title: West Bengal Cabinet expanded to 41 members as 35 BJP MLAs take oath as ministers
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari carried out the first expansion of his Cabinet nearly four weeks after the Bharatiya Janata Party formed its maiden government in the state. On Monday, 35 B...

item.title: Israel highlights India’s growing role in West Asia stability and economic integration
item.description: Israel has emphasised India’s increasing strategic and economic importance in the West Asia region, stating that New Delhi has a significant role to play in promoting stability and long-term prosperit...

item.title: Nigeria Needs Domestic Refining To Strengthen Economy And Reduce Import Dependence
item.description: The Africa Energy Chamber (AEC) don advise say Nigeria suppose put more mind for domestic refining to reduce how the country dey suffer from imported petroleum shocks. According to NJ Ayuk, the Chairm...

item.title: China’s Surveillance Technology Expands Across African Cities
item.description: Across cities like Dakar, Lagos and Abidjan, it is becoming hard to waka freely without being captured on camera. Most of these cameras and the complex systems behind them are supplied by Chinese comp...

item.title: Macron Secures Record €93bn in Foreign Investments to Boost French AI and Data Centre Industry
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron don announce say France don secure €93 billion ($108 billion) foreign investment at this year international 'Choose France' conference. This one pass the combined €87 billion...

item.title: Apple outlines iOS 27 rollout timeline with developer beta in June and general release in September 2026
item.description: The article explains Apple’s expected rollout schedule for iOS 27, the next major iPhone operating system update. According to the established annual pattern, Apple will officially unveil iOS 27 durin...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo reaches 282 confirmed cases as survivors share recovery experiences
item.description: Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo are continuing to respond to a growing Ebola outbreak, with confirmed cases now reaching 282. The situation remains a significant public health c...

item.title: Senate Democrats move to block $1.8 billion Justice Department fund tied to Trump administration disputes
item.description: Senate Democrats have launched a coordinated effort to dismantle a $1.8 billion Justice Department program that critics describe as a politically motivated “anti-weaponization” fund linked to Presiden...

item.title: Dodoma Becomes Tanzania’s Leading Market for Commercial Property Rents
item.description: Dodoma, the administrative capital of Tanzania, is quickly becoming the country's top city for commercial property. New data show that office rents in Dodoma now range between Sh30,000 and Sh35,000 pe...

item.title: The Risks of Overreliance on AI in Writing and Politics
item.description: Nesrine Malik argues that while artificial intelligence offers convenience and speed, it fundamentally lacks meaning and the human touch essential for authentic expression. She recounts incidents wher...

item.title: Emmy Stikxx Shapes Bradford’s Cultural Scene Through African Percussion
item.description: This report highlights how Nigerian-born percussionist Emmanuel “Emmy Stikxx” Ifejika has become a major force in Bradford’s growing cultural movement, especially during the city’s 2025 UK City of Cul...

item.title: Ruto names Wajir Stadium after Ahmed Khalif during historic Madaraka Day in Northern Kenya
item.description: President William Ruto marked a historic Madaraka Day celebration in Wajir County, the first time the national event has been held in Northern Kenya in 63 years. Speaking during the celebrations on Ju...

item.title: South African Parliament Convenes First Impeachment Inquiry Panel into President Ramaphosa While Johannesburg Faces Service Strains
item.description: For the first time in South Africa’s democratic history, Parliament has convened a 31-member committee to consider possible impeachment proceedings against a sitting president. The panel, established ...

item.title: Musoma Youth Raise Concerns Over Inaccessible Government Loans and Lack of Local Accountability
item.description: Youth in Musoma, Mara Region, are expressing strong frustration and loss of trust in local government due to poor access to empowerment loans and weak leadership accountability. During a community dia...

item.title: Canberra duo Jason Geria and Kai Trewin named in Socceroos World Cup squad
item.description: Two Canberra-produced footballers, Jason Geria and Kai Trewin, have earned selection in Australia’s final 26-man Socceroos squad for the FIFA World Cup after a remarkable journey from local junior foo...

item.title: Japan and South Korea stocks reach record highs as oil prices rise amid fragile Iran ceasefire optimism
item.description: Stock markets in Japan and South Korea reached new record highs as investors reacted to shifting global geopolitical and energy market conditions. According to the Associated Press, Asian equities con...

item.title: Class Action Lawsuit Alleges Amazon’s Subscribe & Save Program May Lead to Higher Prices for Some Customers
item.description: A new proposed class-action lawsuit filed in U.S. District Court for Western Washington accuses Amazon of misleading customers through its “Subscribe & Save” program. The program, designed to encourag...
